# Joshua Garst — Full-Stack Software Engineer

> Plain-text, AI-friendly summary of Joshua Garst's background, experience, and projects. Sourced from the canonical content at https://joshua-garst-portfolio-website.netlify.app/.

## Snapshot

- **Name:** Joshua Garst
- **Role:** Full-Stack Developer II at Sharp Hue (Fayetteville, Arkansas)
- **Location:** Bentonville, Arkansas (Northwest Arkansas)
- **Professional experience:** 3+ years as a software engineer
- **Primary stack:** .NET, C#, ASP.NET MVC, Razor Pages, JavaScript, React
- **Secondary stack:** TypeScript, Node.js, Python, Django, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, SQL Server
- **Cloud & ops:** Microsoft Azure (Functions, Blob Storage, Application Insights), Docker, CI/CD pipelines
- **Email:** joshuagarst@gmail.com
- **LinkedIn:** https://www.linkedin.com/in/joshua-garst/
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/testler
- **Resume (PDF + DOCX):** https://joshua-garst-portfolio-website.netlify.app/resume

## Professional Experience

### Sharp Hue — Software Developer II (Fayetteville, AR)

Joshua joined Sharp Hue as an apprentice after completing a six-month coding boot camp in 2021 while working full-time. He has since worked up to Software Developer II. His work focuses on:

- Building and maintaining enterprise CRM systems on Microsoft Dynamics 365
- Designing and shipping .NET Minimal APIs and Azure Functions
- Developing data-driven internal tools used by real clients in production
- Azure infrastructure work: Blob Storage for document management, Application Insights for observability
- CI/CD pipeline design for reliable deployments
- Server-rendered web development with ASP.NET MVC and Razor Pages
- Frontend work with JavaScript, jQuery, Knockout.js, and modern React

## Technical Skills (detailed)

**Languages:** C#, JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, SQL, HTML5, CSS3, PHP

**Backend frameworks:** ASP.NET MVC, .NET Minimal APIs, Entity Framework, Node.js, Express, Django, Spring Boot

**Frontend frameworks / libraries:** React, Three.js, React Three Fiber, jQuery, Knockout.js, EJS

**Databases:** Microsoft SQL Server, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite

**Cloud (Azure):** Azure Functions, Azure Blob Storage, Azure Application Insights

**Enterprise platforms:** Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM

**Tooling & ops:** Docker, CI/CD Pipelines, Git, GitHub, Visual Studio, VS Code, Vite, Webpack

## Personal Projects

### CyberDefense — Browser tower defense game
- **Stack:** Vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript (no framework, no build step)
- **Why:** Learn DOM-level game loop mechanics, collision detection, and state management without leaning on abstractions
- **Live:** Playable on the site

### Date Night Organizer — Full-stack scheduling app
- **Stack:** Node.js, Express, EJS server-rendered views, MongoDB
- **Why:** Build a useful tool for real-life logistics while exercising traditional server-rendered MVC patterns

### Movie App — MERN stack project
- **Stack:** MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js
- **Why:** Round out experience with the canonical full-stack JavaScript workflow

### The Cube — 3D interactive experiment
- **Stack:** React Three Fiber on top of Three.js
- **Why:** Explore declarative 3D rendering in React; foundations for the interactive dodecahedron that powers this portfolio's home page

### This Portfolio Site
- **Stack:** React 19, Vite, React Three Fiber, React Router, react-helmet-async, deployed on Netlify
- **Why:** Demonstrate interactive graphic-novel UX, client-side 3D rendering, and content-driven architecture. Content and dialogue live in plain JavaScript config so site copy can be updated without touching component code.

## Background

Joshua transitioned into software engineering through a coding boot camp in 2021 after careers in more hands-on work. His non-software experience includes farming and regenerative agriculture. He still helps with his grandfather's orchard in Gravette, Arkansas, and maintains a small backyard organic garden that he plans to expand with a greenhouse and DIY ground-mount solar array in the future.

He lives in Bentonville, Arkansas with his family.

## Engineering Philosophy

- Favors simple, readable code over clever abstractions
- Prefers editing existing patterns to inventing new ones
- Believes bug fixes should not drag refactors along with them
- Writes comments only when the *why* is non-obvious; trusts well-named identifiers to explain the *what*
- Values shipping over perfection, but not over correctness

Preferred contact is email: **joshuagarst@gmail.com**

Response time: reads email within a day, replies within two. LinkedIn DMs also work.
